english: pa; also for to fetch, to go for, if the object is of a sacred character, e.g. relics; spyan ma dráṅs-par ₒgró-ba (spyan ma drangs par 'gro ba) to go uninvited Cs. -- 5. to place before one, to serve up, dishes, meals; to pour out, beer, wine etc., ccdpar., frq.; resp. with żál-du (zhal du) Pth.; to taste, to eat or drink what has been offered, resp. W. (cf. mčód-pa (mchod pa), gsól-ba (gsol ba)). -- 6. to count, to number, esp. with re (re), or re-ré-nas (re re nas), separately, one by one, Glr., Mil.; to enumerate, ma draṅs (ma drangs) ... are here not enumerated Wdn.; c. termin. to count for, to consider, to look upon as, dpé-ru (dpe ru) as a parable, as not existing Mil. -- 7. W. in a general sense: to convey, to remove, *zá-ċe túr-maṅ naṅ den* (za ce tur mang nang den) food is conveyed by a spoon, *ḳa ḳyem daṅ den* (kha khyem dang den) snow is removed by a shovel. -- 8. further: rkaṅ (rkang) (resp. żabs (zhabs)) ₒdrén-pa ('dren pa) to insult, to scoff, to deride Thgy., C. -- me ₒdrén-pa (me 'dren pa) the blazing, flaring of a flame Sch. -- mgo-ₒdren (mgo 'dren) v. mgo (mgo), comp.
mixture, medley, e.g. in border-districts a mixed dialect, a mixed religion; a mixed colour, e.g. gray.

= ₒdrén-pa ('dren pa), esp. in conjunction with spyan (spyan): spyán-ₒdroṅs-sam ltos śig (spyan 'drongs sam ltos shig); mi ₒdróṅs-na (mi 'drongs na) try whether you can invite him (whether he will come); if not, then ... Mil., also Mil. nt.

1. to wince, shrink, quiver, start, from fear; to shy, of horses; ₒdróg-can ('drog can) shy, skittish, easily frightened W. -- 2. -- ₒdrog-slóṅ-ba ('drog slong ba) Sch.: to take by surprise, to deceive by cunning, to outwit; blo-ₒdróg (blo 'drog) Lex. w.e.

pf. and fut. brdal (brdal), imp. rdol (rdol), also ydál-ba (gdal ba), bdál-ba (bdal ba), 1. to spread, sand, stones, manure, esp. done by means of a stick, rake, shovel etc.; to extend, a canopy Pth.; to cover, rdziṅ-gi żabs byé-mas (rdzing gi zhabs bye mas), the bottom of a pond with sand Dzl.; fig. dam-čos taṅ-mar bdál-ba-la (dam chos thang mar bdal ba la) now when holy religion lies before you as if it were spread out in a plain, i.e. when it is accessible to all, Mil.; kyab-ydál (khyab gdal) or rdál (rdal) spreading far and wide, all-embracing, sems nám-mka ltá-bu (sems nam mkha' lta bu), čos-kyi kloṅ (chos kyi klong), čos-dbyiṅs (chos dbyings), and the like; groṅ-rdál (grong rdal) v. sub groṅ (grong). -- 2. sos-ydál (sos gdal) Lex. w.e.; Sch.: slowly, not in a hurry.
= yo-byád? (yo byad?) náṅ-gi rdig kun (nang gi rdig kun) Mil. seems to mean: all the utensils and furniture of a house.
to beat Sch., prob. = rdég-pa (rdeg pa).

pf. rdibs (rdibs), vb. n. to rtib-pa (rtib pa), to fall to pieces, to give way, to break down, of a roof, rock, tree, the heavens. -- 2. to get dinted, battered, like tin-vessels by a blow or knock, C., W.
Cs. thistle, not generally known, but perh. the same as ma-rdu (ma rdu).

pf. brdugs (brdugs), fut. brdug (brdug), 1. to conquer, to vanquish (?), klú-rnams-kyis lha-ma-yin túb-ċiṅ rdúg-par byás-te (klu rnams kyis lha ma yin thub cing rdug par byas te) the Nagas having overcome and vanquished the Asuras Stg.; hence prob. to annihilate, destroy, undo, der tabs brdugs-pas (der thabs brdugs pas) as all resources were destroyed Pth. -- 2. to strike against, to stumble at, C. (cf. túg-pa (thug pa) II, 3); togs-rdug (thogs rdug) (or brtug (brtug))-méd-pa (med pa), v. tógs-pa (thogs pa), without impediment.

pf. brduṅs (brdungs), fut. brduṅ (brdung), imp. (b)rdúṅ(s) ((b)rdung(s)), also bdúṅ-ba (bdung ba), to beat, to strike, a person, a drum etc.; to cudgel, to drub, also rdúṅ-ₒtsog-pa (rdung 'tsog pa) (Sch. -ₒtsob-pa? (-'tsob pa?)); to beat with a hammer, to hammer, lcags (lcags); rdúṅ-du rúṅ-ba (rdung du rung ba) malleable, ductile; to knock, sgo (sgo) at a door; to break to pieces, to smash, rdo-yis (rdo yis) with a stone (the sacrificial vessels) Glr.; to beat out, brá-bo (bra bo) buckwheat, with a stick; hence to beat out with a flail, to thrash; to pound, to bray; stéṅ-rduṅ (steng rdung) a pestle Ld. -- bro rdúṅ-ba (bro rdung ba) to dance. -- yżu rdúṅ-ba (gzhu rdung ba) to bend the bow, v. Schf. on Dzl. 252, 11. -- rdúṅ-mkan (rdung mkhan) a fighter, bully; of horses:
